methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $17M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Proteus Space is a Series A aerospace company founded by David Kervin (20+ years in US military government contracting) and Andrew Shapiro (20 years at NASA JPL, Manager of Technology Formulation; led 3D-printed component development for Mars Perseverance Rover). The company has raised $14.4M to date. The Space & Aerospace sector peer universe includes 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Only one valuation method was applicable to this analysis. Stage-Progression methodology uses a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, applied to a $17M base value derived from total capital raised. This approach yields an implied valuation of $34M. Peer-median analysis was inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the narrow Space & Aerospace set. Public-comp anchoring using the sector median P/S of 25.8x could not be executed absent the target's revenue disclosure, which is standard among private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $29M to $39M, with a median of $34M. This range reflects confidence in founder pedigree and relevant technical expertise but remains heavily dependent on stage-progression methodology. Key uncertainties include undisclosed revenue run-rate, customer traction, technology differentiation versus the private peer set, and contract pipeline visibility—all material to refined valuation but not available in current data.
